Cough.Definition: A disorder characterized by sudden, often repetitive, spasmodic contraction of the thoracic cavity, resulting in violent release of air from the lungs and usually accompanied by a distinctive sound.
Cough.Grade 2: Moderate symptoms, medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Cough.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL

Dyspnea.Definition: A disorder characterized by an uncomfortable sensation of difficulty breathing.
Dyspnea.Grade 1: Shortness of breath with moderate exertion
Dyspnea.Grade 2: Shortness of breath with minimal exertion; limiting instrumental ADL
Dyspnea.Grade 3: Shortness of breath at rest; limiting self care ADL

Hypoxia.Definition: A disorder characterized by a decrease in the level of oxygen in the body.
Hypoxia.Grade 2: Decreased oxygen saturation with exercise (e.g., pulse oximeter <88%); intermittent supplemental oxygen
Hypoxia.Grade 3: Decreased oxygen saturation at rest (e.g., pulse oximeter <88% or PaO2 <=55 mm Hg)
Hypoxia.Grade 4: Life-threatening airway compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)

Pneumonitis.Definition: A disorder characterized by inflammation focally or diffusely affecting the lung parenchyma.
Pneumonitis.Grade 1: Asymptomatic; clinical or diagnostic observations only; intervention not indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 2: Symptomatic; medical intervention indicated; limiting instrumental ADL
Pneumonitis.Grade 3: Severe symptoms; limiting self care ADL; oxygen indicated
Pneumonitis.Grade 4: Life-threatening respiratory compromise; urgent intervention indicated (e.g., tracheotomy or intubation)
